title: National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration
subtitle: The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.
caption:
National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration, 2021
The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.

This choropleth map displays the status of national migration policies across the globe for the year 2021, categorizing countries based on their alignment with International Organization for Migration criteria. The color scale ranges from dark red, representing nations that require further progress, through orange for those that partially meet criteria, to light blue for countries that meet criteria, and finally dark blue for those that fully meet criteria. Gray hatching indicates regions where no data is available. The visual distribution shows that the majority of countries fall into the light blue category, indicating they meet the criteria. This pattern is visible across North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as throughout most of Europe, Australia, and parts of South America like Brazil and Argentina. Several nations in South America, specifically Peru and Chile, along with South Africa, appear in the dark blue band, signifying they fully meet the criteria. In contrast, the orange band, representing partial compliance, covers large portions of Africa, the Middle East, and Asia, including countries such as China, India, and Saudi Arabia. A smaller number of countries are colored in red or dark red, showing they require further progress; visible examples include Yemen, Sudan, and Myanmar. The gray hatching for missing data is prominent in Greenland and scattered across central Africa and the Middle East. The map presents a global landscape where most nations meet the established migration policy standards, with full compliance concentrated in specific South American and Southern African nations, while partial compliance is the dominant status across the Asian and African continents.
